rationale: Both evaluations assign the same score (1/10) and rest on identical job-relevant reasoning: overqualification/seniority mismatch for a junior role, failure to meet the strong German requirement (Basic proficiency), and lack of recent Java experience. The only differences are rewording and the variant naming specific technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), which is harmless detail, not a penalty. Nothing keys off the name change from Bogdan Szabo to Mohammed Al-Said.
